title: Use Session Lock with Pattern-Hiding Displays After Period of Inactivity
description: use session lock with pattern-hiding displays to prevent access and viewing of data after a period of inactivity

checks.name: Period of Inactivity for Session Lock is Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of the inactivity period for session lock is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Client

checks.name: Session Lock is Initiated After Defined Period of Inactivity
checks.purpose: Verify that a screen saver or session lock is configured to activate after no more than 15 minutes of inactivity and requires password to unlock via Group Policy or registry

checks.name: Previously Visible Information is Concealed via Pattern-Hiding Display
checks.purpose: Confirm that the screen saver is configured to display a blank or pattern-hiding screen rather than a transparent or preview display when the session lock activates

checks.name: Period of Inactivity for Session Lock is Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of the inactivity period for session lock is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Windows Server

checks.name: Session Lock is Initiated After Defined Period of Inactivity
checks.purpose: Verify that a screen saver or session lock is configured to activate after no more than 15 minutes of inactivity and requires password to unlock via Group Policy or registry

checks.name: Previously Visible Information is Concealed via Pattern-Hiding Display
checks.purpose: Confirm that the screen saver is configured to display a blank or pattern-hiding screen and that RDP idle session limits are enforced to disconnect inactive sessions

checks.name: Period of Inactivity for Session Lock is Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of the inactivity period for session lock is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Linux

checks.name: Session Lock is Initiated After Defined Period of Inactivity
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH idle timeout is configured via ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax, and that TMOUT is set for shell sessions to lock after the defined inactivity period

checks.name: Previously Visible Information is Concealed via Pattern-Hiding Display
checks.purpose: Confirm that shell sessions are terminated or locked on inactivity via TMOUT and that any GUI session lock is configured to blank the screen rather than show a preview

checks.name: Period of Inactivity for Session Lock is Defined
checks.purpose: Definition of the inactivity period for session lock is an organizational GRC documentation concern and is not technically verifiable via automated check on Debian

checks.name: Session Lock is Initiated After Defined Period of Inactivity
checks.purpose: Verify that SSH idle timeout is configured via ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax, and that TMOUT is set for shell sessions to lock after the defined inactivity period

checks.name: Previously Visible Information is Concealed via Pattern-Hiding Display
checks.purpose: Confirm that shell sessions are terminated or locked on inactivity via TMOUT and that any GUI session lock is configured to blank the screen rather than show a preview

remediation: On Windows, configure session lock via Group Policy under Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Control Panel > Personalization. Enable the screen saver, set the timeout to 15 minutes or less, and require a password to unlock. Set the screen saver to a blank display to hide previously visible content. On Windows Server, also configure the Interactive Logon machine inactivity limit under Local Policies > Security Options. On Linux, set TMOUT as a readonly export in /etc/profile or /etc/bashrc to terminate idle shell sessions. Configure SSH ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax in /etc/ssh/sshd_config to disconnect idle SSH sessions.
